fields.Text:
A radiologist is reading a restaging positron emission tomography (PET) computed tomography (CT) scan on a 39-year-old female with a history of non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma?

{{c1::Use of colony-stimulating factors}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The sagittal positron-emission tomography (PET) shows diffuse marrow uptake of fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG) throughout the spine and sternum (red arrows) as well as several sites of FDG uptake in enlarged abdominal lymph nodes (yellow arrows). Correct answer: Use of colony-stimulating factors.

fields.Text:
An 11-year-old male presents for evaluation of epilepsy. Which is true?

{{c1::The abnormal findings have a strong correlation with intractable epilepsy.}}

1/8
fields.Extra: Q2. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Periventricular nodular heterotopia (PVNH) represents normally organized neurons in the wrong location due to migration failure, maintaining gray matter signal characteristics on all MRI sequences. PVNH is associated with seizure and developmental risks. Correct answer: The abnormal findings have a strong correlation with intractable epilepsy..

fields.Text:
Which concerning tracheobronchial injury from blunt chest trauma is correct?

{{c1::More commonly involves the right bronchial tree}}
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: hard. diagram of tracheobronchial tree Anatomic configuration of the right bronchial tree makes it preferentially more susceptible to injury compared to the left. Correct answer: More commonly involves the right bronchial tree.

fields.Text:
A 59-year-old male presents with tearing chest pain. A single image from a "double rule-out" chest CTA is provided below. The most accurate diagnosis in this case is:

{{c1::aortic aneurysm rupture.}}
fields.Extra: Q4. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Aneurysmal descending thoracic aorta with mediastinal hematoma and hemothorax represents contained rupture requiring emergent intervention. Correct answer: aortic aneurysm rupture..

fields.Text:
A 42-year-old female who has Peutz-Jeghers has a finding while undergoing surveillance imaging. The accompanying MRI was performed. Which is true regarding the findings?

{{c1::The finding is associated with a very poor prognosis.}}

1/7
fields.Extra: Q5. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Adenoma malignum is a rare form of cervical cancer that is associated with Peutz-Jeghers syndrome. Correct answer: The finding is associated with a very poor prognosis..

fields.Text:
Which options names the radiographic findings shown?

{{c1::Boxer’s fracture}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q6. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Boxer fracture involves the distal fifth metacarpal with volar angulation. Correct answer: Boxer’s fracture.

fields.Text:
A 16-year-old male adolescent is complaining of persistent hip pain. The x-ray and bone scan are seen below. Which is the most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Ewing sarcoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The triad of adolescent age, permeative bone destruction, and large soft tissue component (evidenced by bone scan soft tissue uptake) is highly characteristic of Ewing's sarcoma, representing the classic presentation of this aggressive primary bone malignancy in its most common demographic and anatomic location. Correct answer: Ewing sarcoma.

fields.Text:
Which is the most appropriate classification for the fracture above? Is the finding generally stable or unstable?

{{c1::Type II odontoid fracture. The fracture is unstable.}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Type II dens fractures are unstable with high nonunion rates. Risk factors for nonunion include older age, displacement, and comminution. Surgical fixation is often required. Correct answer: Type II odontoid fracture. The fracture is unstable..

fields.Text:
A 21-year-old female patient presents to the emergency department (ED) with right lower quadrant pain that has been present for a week and is getting progressively?

{{c1::Corpus luteum}}
fields.Extra: Q9. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Corpus luteum appears as a thick-walled cyst ≤3cm with characteristic peripheral vascularity on Doppler. Peripheral flow pattern distinguishes it from solid ovarian lesions which show internal vascularity (O-RADS 1 when physiologic). Correct answer: Corpus luteum.

fields.Text:
A 15-year-old boy presents to his pediatrician for left distal thigh pain for the past 4 months. The pain worsens, causing him to walk with a slight limp and have?

{{c1::Overproduction of osteoid cells by malignant cells.}}
fields.Extra: Q10. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Osteosarcoma represents approximately 20% of all primary malignant bone tumors. It tends to occur in the metaphysis and is caused by the unchecked overproduction of osteoid cells. Prior radiation or chemotherapy are risk factors. A pathognomonic finding on X-ray is the “Codman triangle,” where the tumor raises the periosteum away from the bone. Correct answer: Overproduction of osteoid cells by malignant cells..

fields.Text:
Newborn presents with posterior neck mass on delivery. The MR images shown were obtained. Which is the most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Chiari 3 malformation}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q11. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Sagittal T1 MR demonstrates inferior translation of the cerebellar vermis (not shown), vertically-oriented towering cerebellum (white arrow), tectal beaking (left image, arrowhead), wrapping of the cerebellum around the midbrain (right image, arrowheads), and Correct answer: Chiari 3 malformation.

fields.Text:
Which is most likely to be present with the fetal anomaly shown here?

{{c1::Polyhydramnios}}
fields.Extra: Q12. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The anomaly shown here is anencephaly, which is the most severe neural tube defect. Correct answer: Polyhydramnios.

fields.Text:
A 56-year-old post-menopausal female patient undergoes an ultrasound because of pelvic pain. Which is the likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Lipoleiomyoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: hard. The sagittal ultrasound image of the uterus shows a well-circumscribed, oval, homogeneously hyperechoic mass (yellow arrow) with a partially hypoechoic rim (light green arrows) seen at the uterine fundus (blue arrow). Correct answer: Lipoleiomyoma.

fields.Text:
Which is correct regarding this routine abdominal ultrasound scan?

{{c1::Normal gallbladder with Phrygian cap anomaly}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Phrygian cap is a common benign congenital gallbladder variant appearing as a pointed fundal fold or bend. It is important to recognize as normal anatomical variation to avoid misinterpretation as pathology or cystic lesion. No treatment required for this developmental variant. Correct answer: Normal gallbladder with Phrygian cap anomaly.

fields.Text:
A 35 year old patient presents with left ankle pain. What is the most appropriate next step?

{{c1::MRI of the hindfoot}}

1/5
fields.Extra: Q15. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. A lucency in the subchondral space of the talus is concerning for an osteochondral defect (red arrow). Correct answer: MRI of the hindfoot.

fields.Text:
This obstetric scan shown below reveals a major fetal anomaly. Which is the best next step?

{{c1::Obtain additional sonographic images}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: When proximal lateral ventricle is poorly visualized, reposition transducer to place it in the far field before diagnosing unilateral ventriculomegaly. Bilateral ventricular assessment is essential as apparent unilateral findings often represent technical artifact rather than true asymmetric disease. Correct answer: Obtain additional sonographic images.

fields.Text:
In addition to the injury shown below, which other region should be carefully scrutinized for injury in this 27-year-old patient who fell resulting in anterior?

{{c1::Anteroinferior glenoid}}

1/4
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The axial proton density image shows a large Hill-Sachs deformity (orange arrows) at the posterosuperior aspect of the humeral head, which on the sagittal oblique image is redemonstrated. Correct answer: Anteroinferior glenoid.

fields.Text:
An 81-year-old male treated with amiodarone presents with worsening dyspnea. Based on his history and the CT images provided, what is the most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Organizing pneumonia}}
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Axial and sagittal CT images show patchy bilateral ground-glass and consolidative opacities involving the mid and lower lung zones. Correct answer: Organizing pneumonia.

fields.Text:
Which is the diagnosis for this 34-year-old female with right upper quadrant pain in the setting of recent cholecystectomy?

{{c1::Bile leak}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: easy. The Tc99m-HIDA scan demonstrates prompt hepatic tracer uptake, which then appears in the cholecystectomy bed (red arrow). Correct answer: Bile leak.

fields.Text:
A 45-year-old female patient with family history of breast cancer in their maternal aunt presents with a palpable breast lump. Given the mammographic and ultrasound?

{{c1::BIRADS 5, ultrasound-guided biopsy}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The other BI-RADS assessment categories are as follows: Correct answer: BIRADS 5, ultrasound-guided biopsy.

fields.Text:
A 6-week-old infant is brought to the emergency department for persistent vomiting. He was born at full-term following an uneventful pregnancy and was well until two?

{{c1::Abdominal ultrasound}}
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (IHPS) usually presents at 3-5 weeks of age with nonbilious emesis, which may be forceful. Despite vomiting, the infant is hungry. IHPS can result in dehydration, hypochloremic metabolic alkalosis, and hypokalemia. Ultrasound is the preferred diagnostic test. Treatment is surgical pyloromyotomy. Fluid and electrolyte abnormalities should be corrected before surgery. Correct answer: Abdominal ultrasound.

fields.Text:
What is most commonly associated with this finding during a second trimester ultrasound?

{{c1::Preterm delivery}}
fields.Extra: Q26. Correct; difficulty: easy. This is an example of cervical funneling in cervical incompetence. Correct answer: Preterm delivery.

fields.Text:
Fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG)-positron emission tomography (PET) would be most accurate for which of the following pulmonary nodules?

{{c1::A 10 mm nodule at the left lung apex}}
fields.Extra: Q27. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: FDG-PET is less accurate for evaluation of smaller nodules at the lung bases and more accurate for larger nodules at the apices. Correct answer: A 10 mm nodule at the left lung apex.

fields.Text:
A 62-year-old male patient with a longstanding history of alcohol use and liver failure is seen for increasing abdominal girth. Which is true regarding their condition?

{{c1::Hepatocellular carcinoma screening is recommended for patients with confirmed cirrhosis.}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q28. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The American Association for the Study of Liver Disease (AASLD) promotes screening ultrasound, typically biannually, in patients with an established diagnosis of hepatic fibrosis. Correct answer: Hepatocellular carcinoma screening is recommended for patients with confirmed cirrhosis..

fields.Text:
What is the point of origin of the mass seen in this 10-year-old boy with heavy epistaxis?

{{c1::Sphenopalatine foramen}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance (MR) images show an avidly enhancing mass in the left nasopharynx extending into the sphenoid sinus and middle cranial fossa. Correct answer: Sphenopalatine foramen.

fields.Text:
A 48-year-old female patient with irritability, muscle weakness, sleeping problems, and an enlarged thyroid on clinical exam undergoes the below ultrasound. Which are?

{{c1::↑ T3 ↑T4 ↓TSH}}
fields.Extra: Q30. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Graves' disease shows markedly increased parenchymal vascularity on Doppler ultrasound creating a "thyroid inferno" appearance, with peak systolic velocities in the inferior thyroid artery potentially reaching 120 cm/sec. Primary risk factors include being female, having a family history of autoimmune disorders, smoking, or experiencing major physiological stress such as pregnancy. Correct answer: ↑ T3 ↑T4 ↓TSH.

fields.Text:
A 45-year-old male with a history of chronic alcohol use disorder presents for a screening RUQ ultrasound. They have no prior imaging, history of malignancy, or?

{{c1::Cirrhosis with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC)}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q32. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Isoechoic nodule with a hypoechoic halo seen on a background of cirrhosis is highly suspicious for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). Correct answer: Cirrhosis with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC).

fields.Text:
A sagittal midline image shown is from a pelvic ultrasound performed for routine pregnancy evaluation. By her last menstrual period, she should be 5 weeks 5 days. Which?

{{c1::Higher than expected beta-HCG}}
fields.Extra: Q33. Correct; difficulty: moderate. This is an example of a complete molar pregnancy, which is a form of gestational trophoblastic disease. Correct answer: Higher than expected beta-HCG.

fields.Text:
A 67-year-old male presents with increased claudication in his right ankle. He undergoes ankle-brachial index testing. Which readings represents the lowest risk for?

{{c1::0.92}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q34. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: ABI is calculated by dividing lower extremity pressure by the higher brachial pressure. An ABI less than 0.90 is diagnostic for PAD with 95% sensitivity and 100% specificity. Values greater than 1.40 indicate noncompressible calcified vessels, while normal/borderline range is 1.00-1.40. Correct answer: 0.92.

fields.Text:
A 73-year-old female patient undergoes a bone scan for lower back pain. Which is the most likely underlying process in the scan shown below?

{{c1::Osteoporosis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q35. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The characteristic H-shaped pattern (the “Honda sign”) is diagnostic of a sacral insufficiency fracture. Underlying causes include osteoporosis. Correct answer: Osteoporosis.

fields.Text:
Which is the most likely diagnosis in this 65-year-old female patient presenting with abdominal pain?

{{c1::Lymphoma}}
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Abdominal lymphoma can present with infiltrative mesenteric adenopathy and solid organ masses while characteristically maintaining vascular patency despite encasement. Correct answer: Lymphoma.

fields.Text:
A 13-month-old male patient presents with seizures that are increasing in frequency and port wine stain facial rash. Which is the most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Sturge-Weber syndrome}}

1/4
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Sturge-Weber syndrome shows pial enhancement on post-gadolinium images, typically involving occipital and posterior parietal cortex ipsilateral to facial port-wine stain. Associated findings include cerebral atrophy, subcortical calcification, and prominent deep medullary veins. Correct answer: Sturge-Weber syndrome.

fields.Text:
A 25-year-old male with no history of hip problems presents with hip pain after lifting weights. What is the most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Osteonecrosis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q38. Correct; difficulty: hard. Explanation: Osteonecrosis, also known as avascular necrosis, results from interruption of the blood supply to the bone. Correct answer: Osteonecrosis.

fields.Text:
A 39-year-old female patient with elevated serum calcium undergoes an ultrasound of their neck. Which is the diagnosis?

{{c1::Parathyroid adenoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q42. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Parathyroid adenomas appear as homogeneously hypoechoic oval masses with characteristic polar feeding vessels and peripheral rim vascularity on Doppler ultrasound. Correct answer: Parathyroid adenoma.

fields.Text:
A 28-year-old female patient presents with vulvar hematoma after vaginal delivery. Which is the diagnosis?

{{c1::Internal pudendal artery hemorrhage}}

1/4
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Internal pudendal artery hemorrhage is extremely rare postpartum but can cause massive pelvic hematoma with hemodynamic instability. CT shows large pelvic hematoma; angiography enables embolization. Correct answer: Internal pudendal artery hemorrhage.

fields.Text:
The findings shown here are diagnostic of which of the following?

{{c1::Mazabraud syndrome}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q45. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Mazabraud syndrome shows fibrous dysplasia with "ground glass" appearance on radiographs and T1 hyposignal/T2 hypersignal on MRI, plus intramuscular myxomas that are T1 hypointense and T2 hyperintense. Correct answer: Mazabraud syndrome.

fields.Text:
A 68-year-old female patient with a history of transient ischemic attacks presents for doppler ultrasound evaluation of their carotid arteries. Which is their degree of?

{{c1::<50%}}
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: IAC Vascular Testing updated carotid stenosis criteria, raising the peak systolic velocity threshold for 50% ICA stenosis from 125 cm/sec to 180 cm/sec due to the original SRU threshold being overly sensitive with poor specificity. Correct answer: <50%.

fields.Text:
A 56-year-old male with a history of lymphoma undergoes a restaging PET CT. Which is the basis for the increased uptake seen in the left chest?

{{c1::Attenuation correction artifact}}

1/8
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: easy. The PET and fused PET CT images show intense activity in the left upper chest wall (yellow arrow), which on the CT correlates with the location of a pacemaker (yellow arrow). Correct answer: Attenuation correction artifact.

fields.Text:
A 56-year-old female has been lost to follow-up for several years and presents for a routine screening mammogram. The patient is called back for suspicious?

{{c1::the results are discordant and repeat biopsy and surgical referral is recommended.}}
fields.Extra: Q49. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Pleomorphic calcifications with fibrocystic change represents imaging-pathology discordance requiring repeat biopsy, preferably excisional biopsy as most stereotactic biopsy systems will provide confirmation of calcification morphology on specimen at the time of the biopsy. This discordance pattern has significant upgrade potential to malignancy. Correct answer: the results are discordant and repeat biopsy and surgical referral is recommended..

fields.Text:
The abnormality seen in the voiding cystourethrogram (VCUG) image below is most commonly discovered in which of the following populations?

{{c1::Children with urinary tract infections}}
fields.Extra: Q50. Correct; difficulty: easy. This VCUG above depicts vesicoureteral reflux on the left, which is most commonly discovered in pediatric patients with a history of recurrent urinary tract infections. Correct answer: Children with urinary tract infections.

fields.Text: A radiologist is reading a restaging positron emission tomography (PET) computed tomography (CT) scan on a 39-year-old female with a history of non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma? Answer: {{c1::Use of colony-stimulating factors}}.
fields.Extra:
Q1. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The sagittal positron-emission tomography (PET) shows diffuse marrow uptake of fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG) throughout the spine and sternum (red arrows) as well as several sites of FDG uptake in enlarged abdominal lymph nodes (yellow arrows). Correct answer: Use of colony-stimulating factors.

1/2

fields.Text: Periventricular nodular heterotopia (PVNH) represents normally organized neurons in the wrong location due to migration failure, maintaining gray matter signal characteristics on all MRI sequences. PVNH is associated with seizure and developmental risks. Key answer: {{c1::The abnormal findings have a strong correlation with intractable epilepsy.}}.
fields.Extra:
Q2. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Periventricular nodular heterotopia (PVNH) represents normally organized neurons in the wrong location due to migration failure, maintaining gray matter signal characteristics on all MRI sequences. 1/8

fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: hard. diagram of tracheobronchial tree Anatomic configuration of the right bronchial tree makes it preferentially more susceptible to injury compared to the left. Correct answer: More commonly involves the right bronchial tree.

fields.Text: Aneurysmal descending thoracic aorta with mediastinal hematoma and hemothorax represents contained rupture requiring emergent intervention. Key answer: {{c1::aortic aneurysm rupture.}}.
fields.Extra: Q4. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Aneurysmal descending thoracic aorta with mediastinal hematoma and hemothorax represents contained rupture requiring emergent intervention. Correct answer: aortic aneurysm rupture..

Q5. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Adenoma malignum is a rare form of cervical cancer that is associated with Peutz-Jeghers syndrome. Correct answer: The finding is associated with a very poor prognosis..

1/7

fields.Text: Boxer fracture involves the distal fifth metacarpal with volar angulation. Key answer: {{c1::Boxer’s fracture}}.
fields.Extra:
Q6. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Boxer fracture involves the distal fifth metacarpal with volar angulation. Correct answer: Boxer’s fracture.

1/2

fields.Text: The triad of adolescent age, permeative bone destruction, and large soft tissue component (evidenced by bone scan soft tissue uptake) is highly characteristic of Ewing's sarcoma, representing the classic presentation of this aggressive primary bone malignancy in its most common demographic and anatomic location. Key answer: {{c1::Ewing sarcoma}}.
fields.Extra:
Q7. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The triad of adolescent age, permeative bone destruction, and large soft tissue component (evidenced by bone scan soft tissue uptake) is highly characteristic of Ewing's sarcoma, representing the classic presentation of this aggressive primary bone malignancy in its most common demographic and anatomic location. 1/2

fields.Text: Type II dens fractures are unstable with high nonunion rates. Risk factors for nonunion include older age, displacement, and comminution. Surgical fixation is often required. Key answer: {{c1::Type II odontoid fracture. The fracture is unstable.}}.

Q8. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Type II dens fractures are unstable with high nonunion rates. Risk factors for nonunion include older age, displacement, and comminution. Surgical fixation is often required. Correct answer: Type II odontoid fracture. The fracture is unstable..

Prior radiation or chemotherapy are risk factors. A pathognomonic finding on X-ray is the “Codman triangle,” where the tumor raises the periosteum away from the bone. Key answer: {{c1::Overproduction of osteoid cells by malignant cells.}}.

Q11. Correct; difficulty: moderate. fields.Extra:
Q13. Correct; difficulty: hard. The sagittal ultrasound image of the uterus shows a well-circumscribed, oval, homogeneously hyperechoic mass (yellow arrow) with a partially hypoechoic rim (light green arrows) seen at the uterine fundus (blue arrow). Correct answer: Lipoleiomyoma.

1/2

It is important to recognize as normal anatomical variation to avoid misinterpretation as pathology or cystic lesion. No treatment required for this developmental variant. Key answer: {{c1::Normal gallbladder with Phrygian cap anomaly}}.
fields.Extra:
Q14. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Phrygian cap is a common benign congenital gallbladder variant appearing as a pointed fundal fold or bend. It is important to recognize as normal anatomical variation to avoid misinterpretation as pathology or cystic lesion. No treatment required for this developmental variant. Correct answer: Normal gallbladder with Phrygian cap anomaly.

fields.Extra:
Q15. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. A lucency in the subchondral space of the talus is concerning for an osteochondral defect (red arrow). Correct answer: MRI of the hindfoot.

1/5

Bilateral ventricular assessment is essential as apparent unilateral findings often represent technical artifact rather than true asymmetric disease. Key answer: {{c1::Obtain additional sonographic images}}.

Q16. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: When proximal lateral ventricle is poorly visualized, reposition transducer to place it in the far field before diagnosing unilateral ventriculomegaly. Bilateral ventricular assessment is essential as apparent unilateral findings often represent technical artifact rather than true asymmetric disease. Correct answer: Obtain additional sonographic images.

1/2

fields.Text: The cumulative risk of radiation-induced cancer according to age at exposure for all cancer sites decreases considerably with increasing age at exposure. Key answer: {{c1::Compared to a 30-year-old, a 10-year-old's lifetime cancer risk is approximately 3 times higher for equivalent doses.}}.
fields.Extra: Q17. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The cumulative risk of radiation-induced cancer according to age at exposure for all cancer sites decreases considerably with increasing age at exposure. Correct answer: Compared to a 30-year-old, a 10-year-old's lifetime cancer risk is approximately 3 times higher for equivalent doses..

fields.Text: In addition to the injury shown below, which other region should be carefully scrutinized for injury in this 27-year-old patient who fell resulting in anterior? Answer: {{c1::Anteroinferior glenoid}}.
fields.Extra:
Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The axial proton density image shows a large Hill-Sachs deformity (orange arrows) at the posterosuperior aspect of the humeral head, which on the sagittal oblique image is redemonstrated. Correct answer: Anteroinferior glenoid.

1/4

fields.Text: An 81-year-old male treated with amiodarone presents with worsening dyspnea. Based on his history and the CT images provided, what is the most likely diagnosis? Answer: {{c1::Organizing pneumonia}}.
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Axial and sagittal CT images show patchy bilateral ground-glass and consolidative opacities involving the mid and lower lung zones. Correct answer: Organizing pneumonia.

fields.Text: When comparing PET tracers, the one with lower positron energy will have better spatial resolution due to shorter positron range before annihilation. Fluorine-18 (0.64 MeV) travels 0.35mm in tissue while Rubidium-82 (3.35 MeV) travels 2.4mm, making F-18 superior for detecting small defects requiring optimal resolution. Key answer: {{c1::82Rb will have a worse spatial resolution.}}.
fields.Extra: Q20. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: When comparing PET tracers, the one with lower positron energy will have better spatial resolution due to shorter positron range before annihilation. Fluorine-18 (0.64 MeV) travels 0.35mm in tissue while Rubidium-82 (3.35 MeV) travels 2.4mm, making F-18 superior for detecting small defects requiring optimal resolution. Correct answer: 82Rb will have a worse spatial resolution..

fields.Text: Which is the diagnosis for this 34-year-old female with right upper quadrant pain in the setting of recent cholecystectomy? Answer: {{c1::Bile leak}}.
fields.Extra:
Q21. Correct; difficulty: easy. The Tc99m-HIDA scan demonstrates prompt hepatic tracer uptake, which then appears in the cholecystectomy bed (red arrow). Correct answer: Bile leak.

1/2

fields.Text: The other BI-RADS assessment categories are as follows: Key answer: {{c1::BIRADS 5, ultrasound-guided biopsy}}.
fields.Extra:
Q22. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The other BI-RADS assessment categories are as follows: Correct answer: BIRADS 5, ultrasound-guided biopsy.

1/3

fields.Text: Infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (IHPS) usually presents at 3-5 weeks of age with nonbilious emesis, which may be forceful. Despite vomiting, the infant is hungry. IHPS can result in dehydration, hypochloremic metabolic alkalosis, and hypokalemia. Ultrasound is the preferred diagnostic test. Treatment is surgical pyloromyotomy. Fluid and electrolyte abnormalities should be corrected before surgery. Key answer: {{c1::Abdominal ultrasound}}.
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (IHPS) usually presents at 3-5 weeks of age with nonbilious emesis, which may be forceful. Despite vomiting, the infant is hungry. IHPS can result in dehydration, hypochloremic metabolic alkalosis, and hypokalemia. Ultrasound is the preferred diagnostic test. Treatment is surgical pyloromyotomy. Fluid and electrolyte abnormalities should be corrected before surgery. Correct answer: Abdominal ultrasound.

fields.Text: Annual lens dose limit for adult radiation workers is 15 rem ({{c1::0.15 Sv}}).
fields.Extra: Q24. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Annual lens dose limit for adult radiation workers is 15 rem (0.15 Sv). Correct answer: 0.15 Sv.

fields.Text: BI-RADS 1 or 2 lesions refer to normal or benign findings after assessment of a breast mass with mammography and, in some cases, breast ultrasound. For patients with a BIRADS 1 or 2 lesion, routine annual screening mammography is adequate for follow-up. Key answer: {{c1::Screening mammography in one year}}.
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: BI-RADS 1 or 2 lesions refer to normal or benign findings after assessment of a breast mass with mammography and, in some cases, breast ultrasound. For patients with a BIRADS 1 or 2 lesion, routine annual screening mammography is adequate for follow-up. Correct answer: Screening mammography in one year.

fields.Text: What is most commonly associated with this finding during a second trimester ultrasound? Answer: {{c1::Preterm delivery}}.
fields.Extra: Q26. Correct; difficulty: easy. This is an example of cervical funneling in cervical incompetence. Correct answer: Preterm delivery.

fields.Text: FDG-PET is less accurate for evaluation of smaller nodules at the lung bases and more accurate for larger nodules at the apices. Key answer: {{c1::A 10 mm nodule at the left lung apex}}.
fields.Extra: Q27. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: FDG-PET is less accurate for evaluation of smaller nodules at the lung bases and more accurate for larger nodules at the apices. Correct answer: A 10 mm nodule at the left lung apex.

fields.Text: A 62-year-old male patient with a longstanding history of alcohol use and liver failure is seen for increasing abdominal girth. Which is true regarding their condition? Answer: {{c1::Hepatocellular carcinoma screening is recommended for patients with confirmed cirrhosis.}}.
fields.Extra:
Q28. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The American Association for the Study of Liver Disease (AASLD) promotes screening ultrasound, typically biannually, in patients with an established diagnosis of hepatic fibrosis. Correct answer: Hepatocellular carcinoma screening is recommended for patients with confirmed cirrhosis..

1/2

fields.Text: What is the point of origin of the mass seen in this 10-year-old boy with heavy epistaxis? Answer: {{c1::Sphenopalatine foramen}}.
fields.Extra:
Q29. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance (MR) images show an avidly enhancing mass in the left nasopharynx extending into the sphenoid sinus and middle cranial fossa. Correct answer: Sphenopalatine foramen.

1/2

fields.Text: Graves' disease shows markedly increased parenchymal vascularity on Doppler ultrasound creating a "thyroid inferno" appearance, with peak systolic velocities in the inferior thyroid artery potentially reaching 120 cm/sec. Primary risk factors include being female, having a family history of autoimmune disorders, smoking, or experiencing major physiological stress such as pregnancy. Key answer: {{c1::↑ T3 ↑T4 ↓TSH}}.
fields.Extra: Q30. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Graves' disease shows markedly increased parenchymal vascularity on Doppler ultrasound creating a "thyroid inferno" appearance, with peak systolic velocities in the inferior thyroid artery potentially reaching 120 cm/sec. Primary risk factors include being female, having a family history of autoimmune disorders, smoking, or experiencing major physiological stress such as pregnancy. Correct answer: ↑ T3 ↑T4 ↓TSH.

fields.Text: A 37-year-old patient with a history of thyroid CA comes to the nuclear medicine department for lymphoscintigraphy and sentinel node localization. The patient states? Answer: {{c1::I-131}}.
fields.Extra: Q31. Correct; difficulty: easy. The management of patients who are lactating and breastfeeding an infant arises not infrequently. Correct answer: I-131.

fields.Text: Isoechoic nodule with a hypoechoic halo seen on a background of cirrhosis is highly suspicious for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). Key answer: {{c1::Cirrhosis with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC)}}.
fields.Extra:
Q32. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Isoechoic nodule with a hypoechoic halo seen on a background of cirrhosis is highly suspicious for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). Correct answer: Cirrhosis with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC).

fields.Text: A sagittal midline image shown is from a pelvic ultrasound performed for routine pregnancy evaluation. By her last menstrual period, she should be 5 weeks 5 days. Which? Answer: {{c1::Higher than expected beta-HCG}}.
fields.Extra: Q33. Correct; difficulty: moderate. This is an example of a complete molar pregnancy, which is a form of gestational trophoblastic disease. Correct answer: Higher than expected beta-HCG.

fields.Text: ABI is calculated by dividing lower extremity pressure by the higher brachial pressure. An ABI less than 0.90 is diagnostic for PAD with 95% sensitivity and 100% specificity. Values greater than 1.40 indicate noncompressible calcified vessels, while normal/borderline range is 1.00-1.40. Key answer: {{c1::0.92}}.
fields.Extra:
Q34. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: ABI is calculated by dividing lower extremity pressure by the higher brachial pressure. An ABI less than 0.90 is diagnostic for PAD with 95% sensitivity and 100% specificity. Values greater than 1.40 indicate noncompressible calcified vessels, while normal/borderline range is 1.00-1.40. Correct answer: 0.92.

1/2

fields.Text: The characteristic H-shaped pattern (the “Honda sign”) is diagnostic of a sacral insufficiency fracture. Underlying causes include {{c1::Osteoporosis}}.
fields.Extra:
Q35. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The characteristic H-shaped pattern (the “Honda sign”) is diagnostic of a sacral insufficiency fracture. Underlying causes include osteoporosis. Correct answer: Osteoporosis.

1/2

fields.Text: Abdominal {{c1::Lymphoma}} can present with infiltrative mesenteric adenopathy and solid organ masses while characteristically maintaining vascular patency despite encasement.
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Abdominal lymphoma can present with infiltrative mesenteric adenopathy and solid organ masses while characteristically maintaining vascular patency despite encasement. Correct answer: Lymphoma.

fields.Text: {{c1::Sturge-Weber syndrome}} shows pial enhancement on post-gadolinium images, typically involving occipital and posterior parietal cortex ipsilateral to facial port-wine stain. Associated findings include cerebral atrophy, subcortical calcification, and prominent deep medullary veins.
fields.Extra:
Q37. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Sturge-Weber syndrome shows pial enhancement on post-gadolinium images, typically involving occipital and posterior parietal cortex ipsilateral to facial port-wine stain. Associated findings include cerebral atrophy, subcortical calcification, and prominent deep medullary veins. Correct answer: Sturge-Weber syndrome.

1/4

fields.Text: A 25-year-old male with no history of hip problems presents with hip pain after lifting weights. What is the most likely diagnosis? Answer: {{c1::Osteonecrosis}}.
fields.Extra:
Q38. Correct; difficulty: hard. Explanation: Osteonecrosis, also known as avascular necrosis, results from interruption of the blood supply to the bone. Correct answer: Osteonecrosis.

1/2

fields.Text: After a patient with an unknown penicillin allergy experiences a severe allergic reaction after being given the antibiotic, the hospital implements a plan to anticipate? Answer: {{c1::Resiliency efforts}}.
fields.Extra: Q39. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Human factor engineering is a discipline in which challenges involving health care providers and safely using available medical equipment and technology are explored. Correct answer: Resiliency efforts.

fields.Text: Ultrasound-dete{{c1::CT}}ed pancreatic mass requires immediate contrast-enhanced pancreatic protocol CT as next step. This provides optimal tumor characterization, staging, and vascular assessment. If CT negative with high clinical suspicion, proceed to MRI with DWI or EUS rather than follow-up imaging, as secondary signs may precede visible mass by months.
fields.Extra: Q40. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Ultrasound-detected pancreatic mass requires immediate contrast-enhanced pancreatic protocol CT as next step. This provides optimal tumor characterization, staging, and vascular assessment. If CT negative with high clinical suspicion, proceed to MRI with DWI or EUS rather than follow-up imaging, as secondary signs may precede visible mass by months. Correct answer: CT.

fields.Text: Patients with existing renal dysfunction and a reduced GFR are at higher risk for iodinated contrast-associated nephropathy. The risk can be reduced by using low- or iso-osmolal agents, ensuring hydration before and after the procedure, and avoiding NSAIDs. Key answer: {{c1::Isotonic normal saline}}.
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Patients with existing renal dysfunction and a reduced GFR are at higher risk for iodinated contrast-associated nephropathy. The risk can be reduced by using low- or iso-osmolal agents, ensuring hydration before and after the procedure, and avoiding NSAIDs. Correct answer: Isotonic normal saline.

fields.Text: {{c1::Parathyroid adenoma}}s appear as homogeneously hypoechoic oval masses with characteristic polar feeding vessels and peripheral rim vascularity on Doppler ultrasound.
fields.Extra:
Q42. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Parathyroid adenomas appear as homogeneously hypoechoic oval masses with characteristic polar feeding vessels and peripheral rim vascularity on Doppler ultrasound. Correct answer: Parathyroid adenoma.

1/2

fields.Text: {{c1::Internal pudendal artery hemorrhage}} is extremely rare postpartum but can cause massive pelvic hematoma with hemodynamic instability. CT shows large pelvic hematoma; angiography enables embolization.
fields.Extra:
Q43. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Internal pudendal artery hemorrhage is extremely rare postpartum but can cause massive pelvic hematoma with hemodynamic instability. CT shows large pelvic hematoma; angiography enables embolization. Correct answer: Internal pudendal artery hemorrhage.

1/4

fields.Text: According to The Joint Commission, "the unexpected occurrence involving death or serious physical or psychological injury, or the risk thereof" refers to which of the? Answer: {{c1::Sentinel event}}.
fields.Extra: Q44. Correct; difficulty: hard. According to The Joint Commission, "a sentinel event is an unexpected occurrence involving death or serious physical or psychological injury, or the risk thereof." Serious injury specifically includes loss of limb or function. Correct answer: Sentinel event.

fields.Text: {{c1::Mazabraud syndrome}} shows fibrous dysplasia with "ground glass" appearance on radiographs and T1 hyposignal/T2 hypersignal on MRI, plus intramuscular myxomas that are T1 hypointense and T2 hyperintense.
fields.Extra:
Q45. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Mazabraud syndrome shows fibrous dysplasia with "ground glass" appearance on radiographs and T1 hyposignal/T2 hypersignal on MRI, plus intramuscular myxomas that are T1 hypointense and T2 hyperintense. Correct answer: Mazabraud syndrome.

1/2

fields.Text: IAC Vascular Testing updated carotid stenosis criteria, raising the peak systolic velocity threshold for 50% ICA stenosis from 125 cm/sec to 180 cm/sec due to the original SRU threshold being overly sensitive with poor specificity. Key answer: {{c1::<50%}}.
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: IAC Vascular Testing updated carotid stenosis criteria, raising the peak systolic velocity threshold for 50% ICA stenosis from 125 cm/sec to 180 cm/sec due to the original SRU threshold being overly sensitive with poor specificity. Correct answer: <50%.

fields.Text: The VACTERL association tends to occur sporadically and consists of the following defects: vertebral/vascular, anal atresia, cardiac, tracheoesophageal fistula, renal, and limb. In an infant with VACTERL, associated anomalies and genetic syndromes should be ruled out. Key answer: {{c1::Renal sonogram and echocardiogram}}.
fields.Extra: Q47. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The VACTERL association tends to occur sporadically and consists of the following defects: vertebral/vascular, anal atresia, cardiac, tracheoesophageal fistula, renal, and limb. In an infant with VACTERL, associated anomalies and genetic syndromes should be ruled out. Correct answer: Renal sonogram and echocardiogram.

fields.Text: A 56-year-old male with a history of lymphoma undergoes a restaging PET CT. Which is the basis for the increased uptake seen in the left chest? Answer: {{c1::Attenuation correction artifact}}.
fields.Extra:
Q48. Correct; difficulty: easy. The PET and fused PET CT images show intense activity in the left upper chest wall (yellow arrow), which on the CT correlates with the location of a pacemaker (yellow arrow). Correct answer: Attenuation correction artifact.

1/8

fields.Text: Pleomorphic calcifications with fibrocystic change represents imaging-pathology discordance requiring repeat biopsy, preferably excisional biopsy as most stereotactic biopsy systems will provide confirmation of calcification morphology on specimen at the time of the biopsy. This discordance pattern has significant upgrade potential to malignancy. Key answer: {{c1::the results are discordant and repeat biopsy and surgical referral is recommended.}}.
fields.Extra: Q49. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Pleomorphic calcifications with fibrocystic change represents imaging-pathology discordance requiring repeat biopsy, preferably excisional biopsy as most stereotactic biopsy systems will provide confirmation of calcification morphology on specimen at the time of the biopsy. This discordance pattern has significant upgrade potential to malignancy. Correct answer: the results are discordant and repeat biopsy and surgical referral is recommended..

fields.Text: The abnormality seen in the voiding cystourethrogram (VCUG) image below is most commonly discovered in which of the following populations? Answer: {{c1::Children with urinary tract infections}}.
fields.Extra: Q50. Correct; difficulty: easy. This VCUG above depicts vesicoureteral reflux on the left, which is most commonly discovered in pediatric patients with a history of recurrent urinary tract infections. Correct answer: Children with urinary tract infections.

fields.Text: Periventricular nodular heterotopia is associated with {{c1::intractable epilepsy}}, whereas tuberous sclerosis is the syndrome classically associated with renal angiomyolipomas and cardiac rhabdomyomas.
fields.Extra:
Q2. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Periventricular nodular heterotopia (PVNH) represents normally organized neurons in the wrong location due to migration failure, maintaining gray matter signal characteristics on all MRI sequences. PVNH is associated with seizure and developmental risks. Correct answer: The abnormal findings have a strong correlation with intractable epilepsy..

1/8

fields.Text: Anencephaly and severe neural tube defects cause {{c1::polyhydramnios}} because fetal swallowing is impaired.
fields.Extra: Q12. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The anomaly shown here is anencephaly, which is the most severe neural tube defect. Correct answer: Polyhydramnios.

fields.Text: A talar osteochondral defect seen as a subchondral lucency on radiographs is best evaluated next with {{c1::MRI of the hindfoot}}.
fields.Extra:
Q15. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. A lucency in the subchondral space of the talus is concerning for an osteochondral defect (red arrow). Correct answer: MRI of the hindfoot.

1/5

fields.Text: For the same radiation dose, a 10-year-old has about {{c1::3 times}} the lifetime cancer risk of a 30-year-old.
fields.Extra: Q17. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The cumulative risk of radiation-induced cancer according to age at exposure for all cancer sites decreases considerably with increasing age at exposure. Correct answer: Compared to a 30-year-old, a 10-year-old's lifetime cancer risk is approximately 3 times higher for equivalent doses..

fields.Text: Rb-82 PET has worse spatial resolution than F-18 FDG PET because Rb-82 has {{c1::higher positron energy and longer positron range}}.
fields.Extra: Q20. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: When comparing PET tracers, the one with lower positron energy will have better spatial resolution due to shorter positron range before annihilation. Fluorine-18 (0.64 MeV) travels 0.35mm in tissue while Rubidium-82 (3.35 MeV) travels 2.4mm, making F-18 superior for detecting small defects requiring optimal resolution. Correct answer: 82Rb will have a worse spatial resolution..

fields.Text: The annual occupational dose limit to the lens of the eye is {{c1::0.15 Sv}}.
fields.Extra: Q24. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Annual lens dose limit for adult radiation workers is 15 rem (0.15 Sv). Correct answer: 0.15 Sv.

fields.Text: FDG PET is most accurate for lung nodules that are at least about {{c1::10 mm}} and not at the lung bases.
fields.Extra: Q27. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: FDG-PET is less accurate for evaluation of smaller nodules at the lung bases and more accurate for larger nodules at the apices. Correct answer: A 10 mm nodule at the left lung apex.

fields.Text: An isoechoic liver nodule with a hypoechoic halo in a cirrhotic liver is suspicious for {{c1::hepatocellular carcinoma}}.
fields.Extra:
Q32. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Isoechoic nodule with a hypoechoic halo seen on a background of cirrhosis is highly suspicious for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). Correct answer: Cirrhosis with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC).

1/3

fields.Text: ABI values below 0.90 suggest PAD; among borderline/abnormal choices, {{c1::0.92}} carries the lowest PAD risk.
fields.Extra:
Q34. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: ABI is calculated by dividing lower extremity pressure by the higher brachial pressure. An ABI less than 0.90 is diagnostic for PAD with 95% sensitivity and 100% specificity. Values greater than 1.40 indicate noncompressible calcified vessels, while normal/borderline range is 1.00-1.40. Correct answer: 0.92.
